title: Metastable Coordination Principle
canonical_statement: Under recursive constraint accumulation, persistent adaptive dynamics increasingly favor metastable coordination geometries that preserve recoverable differentiated accessibility across extended horizons.

formal_statement.statement: Localized survivability structures may remain temporarily viable, but recursive constraint burden progressively compresses accessible future-state structure unless recoverable coordination pathways persist.
formal_statement.consequence: Long-horizon adaptive persistence increasingly favors recoverable coordinated differentiation.

one_sentence_lock: Long-horizon adaptive persistence increasingly favors metastable coordination geometries that preserve recoverable differentiated accessibility under recursive constraint.
